# Alan Ruddock

> Associate Professor of Sport Physiology and Performance, Sheffield Hallam University. Co-Founder of Boxing Science. PhD FCASES SFHEA CSci.

## About

Dr Alan Ruddock is an Associate Professor of Sport Physiology and Performance in the School of Sport and Physical Activity at Sheffield Hallam University, UK. He is a Fellow of the Chartered Association of Sport and Exercise Sciences (CASES), a Chartered Scientist (CSci), and a Senior Fellow of the Higher Education Academy (SFHEA). He co-founded Boxing Science, an applied sport science consultancy supporting elite combat sport athletes including world champions.

## Current Roles

- Associate Professor of Sport Physiology and Performance, Sheffield Hallam University (2023 - present)
- Co-Founder, Boxing Science (2013 - present)
- Director & Chair of Physiology and Nutrition Division, CASES (2024 - present)
- Ethics Lead, School of Sport and Physical Activity, Sheffield Hallam University (2019 - present)
- Course Leader, BSc Sport and Exercise Science (managing 350 students and 30 staff)

## Research Impact

- 50+ peer-reviewed publications
- 1,624 citations
- h-index: 25
- i10-index: 31
- >£130,000 in research funding
- 4 completed PhDs, 5 current PhD candidates
- 225,000+ reads on ResearchGate
- CASES Applied Practitioner Award recipient (2021)

## Areas of Expertise

Applied Physiology, Sport Performance, High-Intensity Interval Training (HIIT), Thermoregulation, Strength and Conditioning, Combat Sports Science, Microvascular Function, Velocity-Based Training

## Applied Impact

Led scientific support for boxers in 15 world-title contests, including Olympic Gold medallist and two-time heavyweight champion Anthony Joshua OBE. Provided scientific support for Russ Cook's ("Hardest Geezer") record-breaking run across Africa (2024). Delivered workshops to over 500 coaches across the UK, Europe, North America and Asia through Boxing Science, reaching 30,000 unique users per month.

## Education

- PhD in Sport Physiology, Sheffield Hallam University (2017)
- MSc in Sport Science, Sheffield Hallam University (2007)
- BSc (Hons.) in Sport Science, Sheffield Hallam University (2005)

## Media & Public Engagement

Over 100 media outputs providing expert commentary across broadcast, print and digital platforms including The Times, Wall Street Journal, BBC, Sky Sports, Live Science, Runners World, and The Conversation.
